political.s: BAE Systems USA makes bipartisan PAC contributions through its Political Action Committee, with documented right-leaning emphasis in federal donations (21,000 of 24,000 contributions to Republicans per FEC data). The company also maintains a substantial lobbying presence, spending $3.36 million in 2025 on federal lobbying efforts related to defense contracts and policy.

charity.s: BAE Systems provides grants and sponsorships focused on military support and STEM education. The company offers employee matching gifts programs and community investment in areas where it operates, with particular emphasis on military personnel, veterans, and aerospace/defense-aligned education.
charity.focus: Military support, veterans services, STEM education

environment.s: BAE Systems has committed to net-zero emissions by 2050 with near-term targets to reduce Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 44% by 2030 and Scope 3 by 25%. The company reports science-based targets aligned with SBTi and has implemented renewable energy and energy efficiency initiatives across facilities, receiving an MSCI ESG Rating of AA.

labor.s: BAE Systems has a mixed labor record with serious OSHA violations documented between 2013-2016, including 13 serious and 8 other-than-serious citations with $103,345 in penalties. However, the Norfolk Ship Repair facility was certified as a Star worksite by OSHA for workplace safety excellence, with injury rates 60% below national average.

dei.s: BAE Systems publishes annual DEI Impact Reports demonstrating commitment to diversity initiatives. The company reports women comprise approximately 25% of workforce and 50% of executive team, while people of color represent 25.6% of U.S. workforce. Glassdoor DEI rating is 3.6/5, equal to sector average.

animals.s: Not applicable - BAE Systems is a defense and aerospace contractor manufacturing weapons systems and military platforms, with no involvement in animal testing or consumer products subject to animal testing policies.

guns.s: BAE Systems manufactures advanced military weapons systems including electronic warfare systems, precision strike munitions, naval guns, and artillery systems as a major U.S. defense contractor supplying the Department of Defense.

privacy.s: BAE Systems operates as a defense contractor handling classified national security information with government oversight through a Special Security Agreement. Specific consumer data privacy practices are not readily disclosed; privacy posture reflects government contractor security requirements rather than commercial privacy standards.

execPay.s: Executive compensation data shows average executive pay of approximately $230,000-$237,000 annually. Specific CEO-to-worker pay ratio not disclosed in public sources. Company maintains executive compensation policies tied to performance but detailed ratio information unavailable.

fec.committeeName: BAE SYSTEMS INC. POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (BAE SYSTEMS USA PAC)
